lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਾ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਾ ਕਉ ਹਰਿ ਸੰਤੁਸਟੁ ਹੈ ਤਿਨੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਗਲ ਮੰਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: jinaa gurasikhaa kau har santusatt hai tinee satigur kee gal manee |
lines.english: Those Gursikhs, with whom the Lord is pleased, accept the Word of the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਦੇ ਤਿਨੀ ਚੜੀ ਚਵਗਣਿ ਵੰਨੀ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: jo guramukh naam dhiaaeide tinee charree chavagan vanee |12|
lines.english: Those Gurmukhs who meditate on the Naam are imbued with the four-fold color of the Lord's Love. ||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਕਾਇਰੁ ਕਰੂਪੁ ਹੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਨਕੁ ਨਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh kaaeir karoop hai bin naavai nak naeh |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh is cowardly and ugly; lacking the Name of the Lord, his nose is cut off in disgrace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਧੰਧੈ ਵਿਆਪਿਆ ਸੁਪਨੈ ਭੀ ਸੁਖੁ ਨਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: anadin dhandhai viaapiaa supanai bhee sukh naeh |
lines.english: Night and day, he is engrossed in worldly affairs, and even in his dreams, he finds no peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਵਹਿ ਤਾ ਉਬਰਹਿ ਨਾਹਿ ਤ ਬਧੇ ਦੁਖ ਸਹਾਹਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh hoveh taa ubareh naeh ta badhe dukh sahaeh |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, if he becomes Gurmukh, then he shall be saved; otherwise, he is held in bondage, and suffers in pain. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਾਂਤਿ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਦਰਿ ਸਚੈ ਸੋਭਾ ਪਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: antar saant sadaa sukh dar sachai sobhaa paeh |
lines.english: There is a lasting peace and happiness deep within them; at the Court of the True Lord, they receive honor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਦਾ ਦਰਿ ਸੋਹਣੇ ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਕਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh sadaa dar sohane gur kaa sabad kamaeh |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs always look beautiful in the Court of the Lord; they practice the Word of the Guru's Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਸਹਜੇ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh har naam paaeaa sahaje sach samaeh |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukhs are blessed with the Name of the Lord; they merge imperceptibly into the True Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬਸਿਸਟਿ ਹਰਿ ਉਪਦੇਸੁ ਸੁਣਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh basisatt har upades sunaaee |
lines.english: As Gurmukh, Vashisht taught the Teachings of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦਿ ਜਪਿ ਹਰਿ ਗਤਿ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh prahilaad jap har gat paaee |
lines.english: As Gurmukh, Prahlaad meditated on the Lord, and was saved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਨਕਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh janak har naam liv laaee |
lines.english: As Gurmukh, Janak lovingly centered his consciousness on the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਹਰਿ ਆਪਿ ਲਹਾਈ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: guramukh har bhagat har aap lahaaee |13|
lines.english: The Lord blesses the Gurmukh with devotion. ||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਕਿਨੈ ਪਾਇਆ ਮੇਰੇ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bin gur har naam na kinai paaeaa mere bhaaee |
lines.english: Without the Guru, no one has found the Lord's Name, O my Siblings of Destiny.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਪਰਤੀਤਿ ਨ ਆਈਆ ਸਬਦਿ ਨ ਲਾਗੋ ਭਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kee parateet na aaeea sabad na laago bhaau |
lines.english: One who has no faith in the True Guru, and who does not love the Word of the Shabad,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਸ ਨੋ ਸੁਖੁ ਨ ਉਪਜੈ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਉ ਗੇੜਾ ਆਵਉ ਜਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: os no sukh na upajai bhaavai sau gerraa aavau jaau |
lines.english: shall find no peace, even though he may come and go hundreds of times.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਸਚੇ ਸਿਉ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh sehaj milai sache siau liv laau |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukh meets the True Lord with natural ease; he is in love with the Lord. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਏ ਮਨ ਐਸਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਖੋਜਿ ਲਹੁ ਜਿਤੁ ਸੇਵਿਐ ਜਨਮ ਮਰਣ ਦੁਖੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: e man aisaa satigur khoj lahu jit seviai janam maran dukh jaae |
lines.english: O mind, search for such a True Guru, by serving whom the pains of birth and death are dispelled.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਸਾ ਮੂਲਿ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਹਉਮੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਜਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: sahasaa mool na hovee haumai sabad jalaae |
lines.english: Doubt shall never afflict you, and your ego shall be burnt away through the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂੜੈ ਕੀ ਪਾਲਿ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਨਿਕਲੈ ਸਚੁ ਵਸੈ ਮਨਿ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: koorrai kee paal vichahu nikalai sach vasai man aae |
lines.english: The veil of falsehood shall be torn down from within you, and Truth shall come to dwell in the mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਾਂਤਿ ਮਨਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਇ ਸਚ ਸੰਜਮਿ ਕਾਰ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: antar saant man sukh hoe sach sanjam kaar kamaae |
lines.english: Peace and happiness shall fill your mind deep within, if you act according to truth and self-discipline.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਪੂਰੈ ਕਰਮਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਹਰਿ ਜੀਉ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਰਜਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak poorai karam satigur milai har jeeo kirapaa kare rajaae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, by perfect good karma, you shall meet the True Guru, and then the Dear Lord, by His Sweet Will, shall bless you with His Mercy. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਕੈ ਘਰਿ ਦੀਬਾਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਤਿਸ ਕੀ ਮੁਠੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਜਗਤੁ ਸਭੁ ਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jis kai ghar deebaan har hovai tis kee mutthee vich jagat sabh aaeaa |
lines.english: The whole world comes under the control of one whose home is filled with the Lord, the King.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕਉ ਤਲਕੀ ਕਿਸੈ ਦੀ ਨਾਹੀ ਹਰਿ ਦੀਬਾਨਿ ਸਭਿ ਆਣਿ ਪੈਰੀ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: tis kau talakee kisai dee naahee har deebaan sabh aan pairee paaeaa |
lines.english: He is subject to no one else's rule, and the Lord, the King, causes everyone to fall at his feet.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਣਸਾ ਕਿਅਹੁ ਦੀਬਾਣਹੁ ਕੋਈ ਨਸਿ ਭਜਿ ਨਿਕਲੈ ਹਰਿ ਦੀਬਾਣਹੁ ਕੋਈ ਕਿਥੈ ਜਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: maanasaa kiahu deebaanahu koee nas bhaj nikalai har deebaanahu koee kithai jaaeaa |
lines.english: One may run away from the courts of other men, but where can one go to escape the Lord's Kingdom?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਐਸਾ ਹਰਿ ਦੀਬਾਨੁ ਵਸਿਆ ਭਗਤਾ ਕੈ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਤਿਨਿ ਰਹਦੇ ਖੁਹਦੇ ਆਣਿ ਸਭਿ ਭਗਤਾ ਅਗੈ ਖਲਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: so aisaa har deebaan vasiaa bhagataa kai hiradai tin rahade khuhade aan sabh bhagataa agai khalavaaeaa |
lines.english: The Lord is such a King, who abides in the hearts of His devotees; He brings the others, and makes them stand before His devotees.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਨਾਵੈ ਕੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਕਰਮਿ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਵਿਰਲੈ ਕਿਨੈ ਧਿਆਇਆ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: har naavai kee vaddiaaee karam paraapat hovai guramukh viralai kinai dhiaaeaa |14|
lines.english: The glorious greatness of the Lord's Name is obtained only by His Grace; how few are the Gurmukhs who meditate on Him. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਅਤਿ ਦੁਖੁ ਲਗਾ ਮਰਿ ਜੰਮੈ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: doojai bhaae at dukh lagaa mar jamai aavai jaae |
lines.english: In love with duality, they suffer terrible pain; they die, and are reincarnated, and continue coming and going.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸੇਵੇ ਜਗਤੁ ਮੁਆ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur seve jagat muaa birathaa janam gavaae |
lines.english: Without serving the True Guru, the people of the world are dead; they waste their lives away in vain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਸਟਾ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਹੈ ਫਿਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਜੂਨੀ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: visattaa andar vaas hai fir fir joonee paae |
lines.english: They live in manure, and are reincarnated again and again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਜਮੁ ਮਾਰਸੀ ਅੰਤਿ ਗਇਆ ਪਛੁਤਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak bin naavai jam maarasee ant geaa pachhutaae |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the Name, the Messenger of Death punishes them; in the end, they depart regretting and repenting. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਜਗ ਮਹਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਏਕੁ ਹੈ ਹੋਰ ਸਗਲੀ ਨਾਰਿ ਸਬਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: eis jag meh purakh ek hai hor sagalee naar sabaaee |
lines.english: In this world, there is one Husband Lord; all other beings are His brides.
